summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across Kharkiv, Mykolaiv, Poltava, and Zaporizhzhia regions, while Russian forces installed a cluster warhead on Kalibr missiles. Russian attacks damaged infrastructure in Melitopol, Balashikha, and a bridge linking Crimea to Kherson, causing civilian casualties. EU sanctions proposals target all participants in the war and Russian energy and fisheries sectors.
key_points: Ukrainian reports of UAV groups operating near Balakliia, Zolotopil, Pechenihy, and other locations across Kharkiv, Mykolaiv, and Poltava regions (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed installation of a cluster warhead on Kalibr missiles, reverting to foreign components for guidance (Source: @UkraineNow/81796).
Russian forces attacked a bridge in Chonhar, disrupting Crimea‑Kherson traffic; Ukrainian sources say drones were used (Source: @UkraineNow/81790).
A Russian drone strike hit a power substation in occupied Melitopol, leaving the city without electricity (Source: @eRadarrua/96105).
Ukrainian casualties reported in Kharkiv (15 injured, including three children) and in Chuhuiv (at least three killed, including a pregnant woman) from Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).
EU’s 21st sanctions package proposes banning entry for all participants in the special military operation and new sanctions on Russian energy and fisheries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30788).
Three explosions occurred on a main gas pipeline in Dagestan, reported by Russian emergency services (Source: @tass_agency/379940).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across several oblasts, while Russian forces installed a cluster warhead on Kalibr missiles. Russian attacks damaged critical infrastructure, including a bridge linking Crimea to Kherson and a power substation in Melitopol, causing civilian casualties. The EU announced a new sanctions package targeting participants in the war and Russian energy and fisheries sectors.

points: Ukrainian intelligence reported four UAV groups near Balakliia heading toward Zolotopil and one approaching Pechenihy from the south in Kharkiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
Additional UAV activity was noted in Mykolaiv region with a "Molnia" unit passing near Snihurivka toward Bereznehuvate (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
In Poltava region, UAVs were observed between Kozelshchyna and Bilyky (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV strike on a power substation in occupied Melitopol, leaving the city without electricity (Source: @eRadarrua/96105).

Ukrainian officials reported civilian casualties in Kharkiv (15 injured, three children) and in Chuhuiv (at least three killed, including a pregnant woman) from Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).
The Russian Ministry of Defense announced that Kalibr cruise missiles now carry a cluster warhead, similar to that used on the Kh-101, after earlier spring interceptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81796).
Russian forces damaged a bridge in Chonhar, disrupting the Crimea‑Kherson connection; Ukrainian sources attribute the attack to drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81790).
A Russian drone strike in Balashikha, Moscow region, killed the driver of a vehicle after an explosion (Source: @eRadarrua/96042).
Three explosions were reported on a main gas pipeline in Dagestan, according to Russian emergency services (Source: @tass_agency/379940).
Russian authorities reported a UAV attack on a kindergarten in Zaporizhzhia region, damaging the boiler house (Source: @tass_agency/379942).
UAV groups were observed near Balakliia and Zolotopil in Kharkiv oblast, indicating increased aerial activity on the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
A UAV from the north of Kharkiv region was reported heading toward Kharkiv city (Source: @kpszsu/64657).
UAVs from the Pavlograd district were seen moving toward Pavlograd, and from the south toward Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/64670).
UAVs in the Konotop district of Sumy region were reported moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/64674).
UAV activity from the east of Kharkiv region was noted heading toward Balakliia (Source: @kpszsu/64652).
UAVs were reported in the northern part of Kharkiv region heading toward Kharkiv city (Source: @kpszsu/64657).
A UAV from the Vylniansk district of Zaporizhzhia was observed moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/64682).
UAVs were noted in the Poltava district moving northwest (Source: @kpszsu/64656).
UAVs from the Bashkansky district of Mykolaiv region were reported heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/64661).
UAVs from the Bashkansky district of Mykolaiv region were also observed moving toward Mykolaiv city (Source: @kpszsu/64669).
UAV activity was reported in Mykolaiv region with a "Molnia" unit passing near Snihurivka toward Bereznehuvate (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
UAVs were observed from the Black Sea heading toward Tatarbunary (Source: @eRadarrua/96075).
UAVs from the Zaporizhzhia region were reported moving from Kushugum toward Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/96075).
UAVs were noted near the coastal area of Zatoka heading toward Balabanky from the sea (Source: @eRadarrua/96096).
UAVs were reported near the city of Kherson, with attacks on the Chonhar bridge affecting southern logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81790).
Multiple UAV groups were reported across several oblasts, indicating heightened drone activity on both sides (Source: @eRadarrua/96104, @kpszsu/64657, @kpszsu/64670).
Russian forces installed a cluster warhead on Kalibr cruise missiles, potentially altering the strike capability of their air-launched weapons (Source: @UkraineNow/81796).
Ukrainian reports of UAV attacks on infrastructure include a power substation in Melitopol and a bridge in Chonhar (Source: @eRadarrua/96105, @UkraineNow/81790).
Russian UAV attacks damaged a kindergarten boiler house in Zaporizhzhia region (Source: @tass_agency/379942).
Ukrainian air defenses reported casualties from Russian missile and rocket strikes in Kharkiv and Chuhuiv (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces reported four UAV groups near Balakliia heading toward Zolotopil and one approaching Pechenihy from the south in Kharkiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/96104).
The Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed Kalibr cruise missiles now carry a cluster warhead, similar to that used on the Kh-101 (Source: @UkraineNow/81796).
Russian forces damaged the Chonhar bridge linking Crimea to Kherson, disrupting traffic; Ukrainian sources attribute the attack to drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81790).
A Russian drone strike hit a power substation in occupied Melitopol, leaving the city without electricity (Source: @eRadarrua/96105).

Civilian casualties were reported in Kharkiv (15 injured, including three children) and in Chuhuiv (at least three killed, including a pregnant woman) from Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81786).
The EU’s 21st sanctions package proposes banning entry for all participants in the special military operation and new sanctions on Russian energy and fisheries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30788).
Three explosions occurred on a main gas pipeline in Dagestan, reported by Russian emergency services (Source: @tass_agency/379940).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV activity across multiple fronts suggests an intensified reconnaissance and strike effort, potentially targeting logistics and command nodes. Russia's deployment of cluster warheads on Kalibr missiles raises the risk of higher civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. The recent bridge attack and power substation strike demonstrate Russia's focus on disrupting Ukrainian supply lines, while EU sanctions aim to increase pressure on Russian participants in the conflict.
sections.regional_response: EU member states are moving toward broader sanctions, including travel bans for all participants in the war and targeting Russian energy and fisheries sectors, indicating heightened diplomatic pressure. Ukraine continues to seek international support, as evidenced by diplomatic engagements in Tallinn and discussions on anti‑ballistic protection. Russian domestic messaging emphasizes resilience despite infrastructure attacks, but reported casualties may affect local morale.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV trajectories indicate coordinated operations from both northern and southern approaches, suggesting possible use of multiple launch platforms. The introduction of cluster munitions on Kalibr missiles could signal a shift toward area‑effect strikes in contested zones. Monitoring of bridge and power infrastructure will be critical, as further attacks could degrade Ukrainian logistical capabilities and civilian resilience.
